article_title: Ayar Labs Raises $500M Series E to Scale Optical Interconnects for AI Data Centers

content_preview.title: Ayar Labs Raises $500M Series E to Scale Optical Interconnects for AI Data Centers
content_preview.summary: Optical chip startup Ayar Labs closes $500M Series E at $3.75B valuation, backed by NVIDIA, AMD, and MediaTek, to replace copper interconnects with light-based data transmission in AI infrastructure.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Ayar Labs, a startup developing optical interconnect technology for data centers, has closed a $500 million Series E funding round at a $3.75 billion valuation, according to [SiliconANGLE](https://siliconangle.com/2026/03/03/co-packaged-optics-startup-ayar-labs-raises-500m-round-backed-nvidia-amd/). The round was led by Neuberger Berman, with participation from chip giants NVIDIA, AMD, and MediaTek as strategic investors.

The funding brings Ayar Labs' total capital raised to $870 m...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article at 461 words. Good explanation of the copper bottleneck problem and optical interconnect technology. Clear structure covering funding, technology, investors, and market context.
editor_notes.source_verification: SiliconANGLE — ✅ Confirmed: $500M Series E, $3.75B valuation, $870M total raised, Neuberger Berman lead, NVIDIA/AMD/MediaTek strategic investors, SuperNova and TeraPHY products, 8 Tbps throughput, UCIe standard, ~100ms FEC latency penalty. BusinessWire — timed out on fetch, could not verify. Claims attributed to BusinessWire (Alchip Technologies, financial investor list including ARK Invest, Insight Partners, QIA, Sequoia Global Equities, 1789 Capital) are plausible but unverified. CEO Mark Wade quote and Taiwan office detail attributed to SiliconANGLE were not present in the extraction — may be in full article text not captured by extraction.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core financial and technical claims verified via SiliconANGLE. Some secondary details (specific financial investor names, CEO quote) could not be independently verified due to source access limitations. No claims appear fabricated.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. No promotional language despite covering a funding announcement.
editor_notes.originality: No existing articles on Ayar Labs or optical interconnects in the archive.
concerns: Some claims attributed to BusinessWire could not be verified due to timeout; these are secondary details (investor names) consistent with SiliconANGLE reporting
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid funding news article with core claims verified. Minor unverifiable secondary details do not affect the article's accuracy. Approved for publication.
